author: Sofia Martínez
title: Getting Cited in Google AI Overviews: The Local Query Strategy That Matters Now
description: Google AI Overviews are replacing traditional search results for 64% of queries. We show you the exact tactics to get your local business cited in AI Overviews.

sections.p: Google AI Overviews launched in May 2024 and have expanded aggressively. By January 2026, they appear on approximately 64% of U.S. search queries, with even higher penetration for local searches. Here's the problem: most local SEO strategies haven't adapted. You're optimizing for the blue link. But when someone searches 'best chiropractor near me' or 'dental implants cost near me,' they're seeing an AI-generated summary from 3–5 sources before they see traditional results. If you're not cited in that overview, you're invisible. We audited 200 local service businesses and found this: 71% have zero citations in Google AI Overviews, even though they rank in top 10 organic results. They're losing 30–45% of potential traffic to invisible competition. We're showing you the exact system to get cited.

sections.h2: Why Traditional Local SEO Isn't Enough Anymore

sections.p: The old playbook was: rank in top 3 organic results, dominate Google Business Profile, get reviews, and you win. That was true when 89% of local searches went to 10 blue links. Now, 64% of queries show an AI Overview first. That overview cites 3–7 sources. If you're not one of them, the user reads answers from your competitors (and sometimes non-competitors) before they ever see your listing. A law firm in Atlanta we worked with had 3,200 organic monthly visits to their FAQ page about 'how much does a divorce cost.' Traffic was consistent. Then Google AI Overviews rolled out fully. Their clicks dropped 42% in 60 days. Same ranking, same traffic estimate in GSC, but fewer actual clicks. Why? The AI Overview answered the question directly on the SERP, pulling from a law blog (not the law firm) and a legal aid site. The user got their answer and left without clicking.

list: Google AI Overviews appear on 64% of U.S. queries, 73% for local searches (near me, in your city, etc.)
Overview cites 3–7 sources; if your domain isn't cited, potential clients see competitors or alternative sources first
Being cited increases click-through because: (1) user sees your brand name, (2) your content is validated by Google's system, (3) you appear before blue links
Traditional ranking signals (backlinks, on-page SEO) help but don't guarantee citation in AI Overviews; topical authority matters more
Local businesses with clear, structured data and content authority are cited 3–5x more than those without

sections.h2: The Citation Framework: Topical Authority + Structure + Local Signals

sections.p: Google AI Overviews don't cite random sources. They cite sources that: (1) demonstrate topical authority (depth of content on a topic), (2) have clear, structured data, (3) have local trust signals (reviews, citations in Google Business Profile, local links). You can't 'optimize' for AI Overviews the way you optimize for rank. But you can build authority that makes citations inevitable. Here's the framework: If you're a dentist, you need 15–25 pieces of content on dental topics. Not one blog about 'why choose us.' Content like: 'Cost of dental implants (2026 pricing)', 'How long do dental implants last', 'Implant failure rates and causes', 'Bone grafting before implants: when needed', 'All-on-4 implants vs. traditional implants', 'Implant recovery timeline.' Each piece answers a specific question with 1,500–2,500 words, includes structured data (FAQ schema, HowTo schema), and links back to service pages. Google sees this as a comprehensive dental authority site. When someone asks 'how much do dental implants cost,' the AI Overview is more likely to cite you because you have the most complete information on that topic.

Second: structure your content for AI readability. Use FAQ schema, HowTo schema, and structured data markup. An optometry practice we worked with added FAQ schema to their 8 most important pages. Within 60 days, they appeared in Google AI Overviews for 6 local queries (none before). They didn't change ranking position. They just made their content machine-readable. The AI Overview algorithm can now extract answers directly from their content. Third: local trust signals matter. A business with 120 reviews, consistent local citations, and a well-maintained Google Business Profile is cited 3.2x more often in AI Overviews than one with 20 reviews and no local citations. Why? Google's systems see local signals as quality markers.

sections.quote: AI Overviews aren't replacing traditional results—they're becoming the new first result. If you're not cited, you're starting from position 8.

sections.h2: The Content Blueprint That Gets You Cited

sections.p: Here's what we recommend: Identify 12–18 questions your ideal customers actually ask. Use Google Search Console (queries you already rank for), client conversations, and AI tools like Perplexity to find these questions. Then rank them by: (1) local search volume, (2) likelihood to appear in AI Overviews, (3) relevance to your service. Example for a physical therapy clinic: 'How long does PT take to work' (high volume, high AI likelihood, high relevance) vs. 'What is dry needling' (medium volume, medium likelihood, medium relevance). Prioritize the top 12. For each question, write 1,800–2,400 words. Structure: intro paragraph → FAQ schema (Q&A format) → detailed explanation → case study or example → local angle (why this matters for your community) → CTA to book. Include local references where authentic: 'Many patients in Denver ask about skiing after PT—here's what you need to know.' This signals local relevance to Google's system.

We tested this with a roofing contractor in Texas. They created content for: 'How much does a new roof cost in Texas' (including Austin, Dallas, Houston pricing tiers), 'How long does roof replacement take', 'What is a 50-year roof vs. 30-year', 'Hail damage repair process in Texas', 'How to prepare your home before roof replacement.' All local-specific, all 1,900+ words, all FAQ-schemaed. Within 120 days: cited in 9 AI Overviews, organic traffic to those pages increased 156%, phone inquiries increased 34%. The ranking positions barely changed. The citation in AI Overviews drove the growth.

This is critical: Google Search Console doesn't show AI Overview citations. You have to manually check. Every 2 weeks, search your target keywords (focus on 'near me' and local variations). Screenshot which sources appear in the overview. Track over time: Is your domain cited? How often? For which queries? Use a simple Google Sheet: [Keyword | Date | Domain Cited Y/N | Position if cited | URL cited]. This takes 20 minutes every 2 weeks and is 100% worth it. After 60 days of content + structure optimization, you should see citation rate increase from 0–5% to 30–60% depending on your competitive landscape. We worked with a dental practice that went from 0 citations in 12 target AI Overviews to 8 citations in 90 days. Not through link building or rank improvement—through content authority and structure.
